title: Industrial Safety Basics
description: Universal safety knowledge applied to ALL trades. This context is included in every report structuring call so the AI always has strong safety awareness.
safety_rules: All personnel must wear required PPE at all times: hard hat, safety glasses, steel-toe boots, FR clothing when required
Fall protection (harness + lanyard) required at 6 feet or above. 100% tie-off policy.
Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) required before any work on energized equipment. Zero energy verification mandatory.
Hot work permits required for any welding, cutting, grinding, or spark-producing work.
Confined space entry requires atmospheric testing, entry permit, attendant, and rescue plan.
Excavations over 4 feet deep require shoring, sloping, or shielding. Competent person must inspect daily.
All work requires a Job Safety Analysis (JSA/JHA) reviewed before starting.
Stop Work Authority: Every worker has the right and obligation to stop unsafe work immediately.
Report all incidents, near-misses, and unsafe conditions immediately to supervisor and safety.
Scaffolds must be inspected and tagged (green tag = safe, red tag = do not use).
No work under suspended loads. Maintain safe distance from crane swing radius.
Fire extinguisher must be within 50 feet of hot work. Fire watch required during and 30 minutes after.
Housekeeping: Keep work areas clean, clear walking paths, secure loose materials.
No drugs or alcohol. Fit for duty at all times.
Emergency: Know your muster point, nearest first aid kit, AED location, and emergency phone numbers.
tools_and_equipment: Always inspect tools before use. Remove damaged tools from service.
Power tools must have guards in place. No modifications to safety devices.
Grinders: face shield required in addition to safety glasses. Check wheel for cracks before mounting.
Extension cords: inspect for damage, no daisy-chaining, GFCI protection required.
Ladders: 3-point contact at all times. Do not stand on top 2 rungs. Secure at top.
Lifts (scissor, boom, man lift): trained operators only. Harness required in boom lifts.
Hand tools: use the right tool for the job. No cheater bars on wrenches unless rated.
Torque wrenches: calibrated and within certification date.
Measuring instruments: calibrated and within certification date.
Welding equipment: inspect leads, connections, and gas lines before use.
